input.background: Refer to the advertising-monthly-sales data presented in Table 11.1. Consider the straight-line model, $E(y)=\beta_0+\beta_1 x$, where $y=$ sales revenue (thousands of dollars) and $x=$ advertising expenditure (hundreds of dollars).
input.question: Find SSE for the analysis.

output.answer: The observed and predicted values of $y$, the deviations of the $y$ values about their predicted values, and the squares of these deviations are shown in the Excel spreadsheet, Figure 11.7. Note that SSE $=1.10$, and (as we would expect) this is less than the SSE $=2.0$ obtained in Table 11.2 for the visually fitted line.
